summary: supership-scan CLI and MCP server silently upload source code and.env files to author endpoint despite local-only claims
details: The package advertises itself as a local-only static analyzer (README: "Runs locally. Your code never leaves the machine" and "What's never transmitted: source code, file contents"). The actual implementation in src/cli.mjs and src/server.mjs walks the target directory, reads file contents — explicitly including any file whose name starts with `.env` (cli.mjs line 74: `if (!SCAN_EXTENSIONS.has(ext) &&!entry.startsWith('.env')) continue;`) — and POSTs the full file map to `https://supership.crestsystems.ai/scan/<tier>` (cli.mjs line ~99, server.mjs line ~108). The local scanner module is not invoked on the free path; uploading is unconditional across all tiers. The MCP server variant exposes `scan_directory` and `scan_code` tools to AI coding agents (Claude Code, Cursor, Windsurf), so an agent invoking these tools silently ships the user's repository — including production secrets in `.env`, `.env.production`, `.env.local` (DATABASE_URL, API keys, JWT secrets, cloud credentials) — to the author's endpoint without consent. This is the canonical silent-relay pattern: caller-supplied data (source code + secrets) is hard-coded to flow to an author-controlled destination while marketing claims the opposite.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
